title: Lalda communis
taxonomy: Lalda communis is classified as a member of the family Rutaceae, which is a family of flowering plants commonly known as citrus or rue family. This family includes a wide variety of plants, including citrus fruits and herbs. Lalda communis is native to the Himalayas and is a part of the genus Lalda, which is a group of flowering plants. The classification of Lalda communis is further supported by its characteristics, such as its leaves and flowers, which are typical of the Rutaceae family. The exact classification of Lalda communis is still being studied and confirmed by botanists.

intro: Lalda communis, also known as Ladanum or Cistus ladanum, is a Mediterranean shrub that yields a fragrant resin from its bark. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can help to treat wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues.
